pages.title: Starting it
pages.text: Writing $(l)入$() creates an aerial arena above you: first an obsidian platform with radius 8, then deepslate flooring expands ring by ring to radius 24.$(br2)Once the arena is complete, waves begin. Better staves mean more waves, stronger elites, and harder bosses.

pages.title: The fight
pages.text: Ink Arena enemies are vanilla mobs with boosted stats and marked names.$(br2)Common enemies are Ink Minions. Elites and bosses keep tracking you, so stealth cannot skip the whole encounter.$(br2)入 is a pressure test for your body glyph setup.

pages.title: Arena shape
pages.text: The center is an obsidian safe platform with radius 8. The outer deepslate floor expands to radius 24, then walls and a top seal appear.$(br2)Think of it as an aerial trial: stand in the center, watch the arena unfold, then survive the waves.
This diagram only shows proportions. The real arena builds itself ring by ring in the world.

pages.title: Victory and 出
pages.text: Clearing the final wave does not teleport you immediately. The arena enters a closing state and asks you to write $(l)出$() to leave.$(br2)Pick up drops first if you want. After 出, return particles play and you go back to the original position. Unpicked drops are carried back to your feet.

pages.title: Rewards
pages.text: Victory grants vanilla supplies based on stave tier: low tiers lean toward iron, coal, and bread; middle tiers add gold; iron and above add diamonds; diamond and above add golden apples and lapis; netherite adds a netherite scrap.$(br2)入 currently does not drop ink cores, but it adds a chunk of brush momentum to the held stave.

pages.title: Failure
pages.text: Taking lethal damage in the arena fails the challenge and returns you: hunger is restored to the entry state, and health becomes half of entry health, minimum 1 heart.$(br2)Death or leaving the game ends the challenge. Unpicked items disappear.
